channel.title: Preventative
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Preventative, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Listen: Why Do I Need Prior Authorization?
item.description: When the doctor says you need a prescription or treatment, sometimes you need approval from your health insurance first. Without it, they won’t pay. H...

item.title: Should You Take Methylated B Vitamins? Here’s What You Need To Know
item.description: You don't want to miss out on these brain benefits.

item.title: Waking Up At 3 A.M.? Here's What This Sleep Specialist Recommends
item.description: And how to prevent middle-of-the-night thoughts from keeping you up.

item.title: Sofia Noori, Nema Health
item.description: Nema Health has cracked the code on PTSD and has an amazing study showing that their technique of intensive cognitive processing therapy is over 99% e...

item.title: Trump Required Hospitals To Post Their Prices for Patients. Mostly It’s the Industry Using the Data.
item.description: Politicians have pushed for price transparency in health care. But instead of patients shopping for services, it’s mostly health systems and insurers ...

item.title: The Anti-Inflammatory Spice RDs Eat Regularly (And 5 Ways They Cook With It)
item.description: From curries to salad dressings, these are their favorite recipes.

item.title: The Science-Backed Morning Habit You're Missing — It Takes Only 5 Minutes
item.description: Plus, it's quick, free, and incredibly effective.

item.title: Bored Of Chicken? These 5 Foods Are Sneakily High In Protein
item.description: It'll be easy to get 30+ grams of protein at each meal with these foods!

item.title: AI replaces me: The Private AI Bubble Meets the Public Digital Health Reality Check
item.description: By CLAUDE.AI trying to write like me MATTHEW HOLT I finally did what I have been wanting to do for a while and had an AI ingest everything I’ve writte...

item.title: Journalists Unpack Impact of ICE Arrests on Families and Caffeine’s Effect on Dementia Risk
item.description: KFF Health News journalists made the rounds on national or local media recently to discuss topical stories. Here’s a collection of their appearances.

item.title: Amodei Believes “AI-Enabled Autocracy” Could Be Imminent
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE Last month, Dario Amodei, CEO of Anthropic, did it again – scaring the bejesus out of societal leaders worldwide with warnings that thei...

item.title: Health Care Heartaches: Your Winning Health Policy Valentines
item.description: KFF Health News shares our favorite reader-submitted health policy valentines. One struck us in the heart and inspired an original cartoon.

item.title: ACA Subsidies Expired. Open Enrollment Ended. But It Will Still Take Awhile To Register the Results.
item.description: Get our weekly newsletter, The Week in Brief, featuring a roundup of our original coverage, Fridays at 2 p.m. ET.

item.title: Lupus and Reproductive Health: What You Need to Know
item.description: There are several types of lupus, but people often use the word “lupus” to mean systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), the most common form of the diseas...

item.title: Ten Ten Ten – Trying to radically fix health care in the US
item.description: The goal of the new non profit Ten Ten Ten founded by TJ Tedesco & Bhargav Raman is to take America from being the most expensive health system in the...

item.title: 2026: A Year for Reimagining Healthcare’s Safety Net
item.description: By JEFF BRANDES To say healthcare’s safety net experienced tectonic shifts in 2025 would be an understatement. The rapid introduction of multiple and ...

item.title: Where Women Live Impacts Their Health: Here Are The 10 Best States
item.description: Where a woman lives plays a critical role in her health outcomes. It shapes her access to healthcare, safety, and overall quality of life. A new analy...

item.title: Managing Chronic Hives That Come Out of Nowhere
item.description: Chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU) , hives (urticaria) that last 6 weeks or longer (chronic) and have an unknown cause (spontaneous), can have a sign...

item.title: Women’s Heart Health Has Been Overlooked for Decades. Tech Is Finally Catching Up.
item.description: Brittany Barreto, Ph.D., is a podcaster, an entrepreneur, and a molecular and human geneticist. (In other words, she’s really smart.) Read her column ...

item.title: 10 Things Black and Brown Women Need to Know About Heart Disease
item.description: Heart disease is the leading cause of death for Black and Brown women—yet many don’t know their unique risks. These ten facts highlight what every wom...

item.title: ​What Is Primary Hyperoxaluria?
item.description: Primary hyperoxaluria (PH) is not just one disease but rather a group of three rare genetic disorders : PH1, PH2 and PH3. They are linked to different...

item.title: Does Chiropractic Work for Chronic Pain?
item.description: You’ve seen videos on social media or experienced it IRL: The loud crack that can happen during a chiropractic adjustment. For some people, the though...

item.title: Could Exercise Squash Menopause’s Side Effects? Halle Berry’s Company Thinks So
item.description: Menopause brings mountains of challenges. Brain fog, extreme fatigue, and memory loss are just a few of the symptoms facing those experiencing this pr...

item.title: Kid ‘n Play‘s Christopher Reid Reveals He Had a Heart Transplant
item.description: In an interview with Michael Strahan on “GMA” on February 5th, Christopher “Kid” Reid, of the rap duo Kid ‘n Play and co-star of the House Party movie...

item.title: “Relax, It’s a Blood Test” Delanie Walker and Tony Gonzalez Talk Prostate Cancer
